title: Macro Weekly: No Bottom Signal — Wyckoff Distributions Everywhere
headline: Only 4 Sectors Standing as PE Illiquidity Emerges and Supply Chains Crack
body: Markets closed on their lows Friday with SPY at $634.09, now ~9% off highs and firmly below the 200-day moving average. The historical 15% decline average targets ~6,000 on the S&P. Every major index is printing lower lows and lower highs — DAX, XLF, SNDK, and the S&P itself all showing Wyckoff distribution patterns identical to 2022. Semiconductors are barely holding, with Nvidia clinging to 167 support in a head-and-shoulders formation. Lose these levels and the AI trade unravels.

Only four sectors show strength: oil/gas services (XOP at new highs), telecom (AT&T strongest technically), agribusiness, and hardware — though hardware is cracking with massive dark pool sells on SNDK. This is textbook late-cycle defensive rotation. The MOVE Index continues accelerating upward (no crush = no bottom signal), the dollar is pitting toward a breakout at 130 (bad for equities), and the VIX remains elevated. Cross-correlation across indices — the key bottoming signal — is absent.

The macro backdrop is deteriorating fast. Reports of boots-on-the-ground preparations, ~40+ damaged refineries with half offline for a year, and emergency reserves already exhausting. The barrel decline now equals the 1970s and 2022 crises combined. Three to four major private equity funds are limiting redemptions, and UBS has reportedly frozen a real estate fund for three years. PVC pipe is gone in Australia, fertilizer costs are spiking, and a 30% food price increase is possible within weeks. Historical structure suggests 60-100 days to bottom — we're only 3-4 weeks in.
callout_body:
MOVE Index crushed: ❌ (accelerating up)
Cross-correlation bottom: ❌ (absent)
Dollar weakness: ❌ (pitting toward 130 breakout)
60-100 day structure: ❌ (only ~3-4 weeks in)
S&P higher high above 6,630: ❌

Fear & Greed: 10.2 (Extreme Fear)
SPY: $634.09 | QQQ: $562.58 | VIX: Elevated
Strong: XOP, AT&T, Energy (ATH), Agribusiness
Weak: MAGS, NVDA (167), SNDK, XLF, DAX
action_items: Watch S&P 6,630 — break above = first higher high and potential sentiment shift.
Monitor MOVE Index for crush signal — historically precedes market basing.
Track XLP at 75 — consumer stress canary.
Patience over prediction: buying the V beats buying the dip.
closing_quote: The best trade right now may be doing nothing. Fear is two times more powerful than greed — let the flow confirm before acting.
